## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright, so we got our Facebook business page set up and created, but now we got to create our meta business manager account. This is essentially the kind of the central hub where we'll actually control and launch and manage our ad campaigns ongoing. So I unfortunately have hit the maximum amount of accounts that I can create, so I'm not going to be walking you through step by step showing you and setting up a new account, but I will be doing screen share and still walking you through step by step of how to do this. So before I actually share with you and walk you through this, I want you guys to be aware that there is a possibility that your login and your process of signing up for one of these meta business manager accounts might look different. And the reason why I say that, so that it doesn't catch you off guard, is the fact that at any given point in time, meta can be testing hundreds if not thousands of different iterations of their sign up process, their different layouts, UX, UI design features, etc. So just know that things are always changing, things always look a little bit different, but the process of setting this up has been the same for quite some time. So with that being said, let's dive into it. The first thing that I want you to do is go to business.facebook.com and when you're there, you're going to see this screen right in front of you. Now there are three different things that you can do. You can either login with your Facebook account or login with your Instagram account. What I would recommend is that you actually go and hit create an account and it will take you to the following screen and you'll do the following. If you are currently logged into your Facebook account, the ability to set up a business account and business manager. So you want to put your business name and account name, your name, and then also you want to put your business email. Now this is really important that this is an email that you have access to because meta is going to actually verify that email address. So I've had clients, other people in the past that put a business email they don't have access to or it's not really easy or it might be somebody else's email and then they got to wait a long time to get that access. So send it to an email that you actually control and you own and you can go in and actually verify that this is your account that you're setting up. So you can put this information in and then the next thing that you're going to go to is this page where you're going to come in and actually add in the legal name of the business as shown on official documents. This is really important that you put the legal name of the business because if you don't have a legal name of the business, you might have issues if you need to verify your business on the path. Now you don't have to verify your business in order to actually set up, run and optimize and launch campaigns but it does help and sometimes if you come up with some sort of an issue like a verification issue or maybe your account gets hacked which has happened in the past for a lot of people, being able to actually verify your business and have everything line up can just be a lot easier and seamless down the path. Add in your street address, put in your city, state, zip, business phone number, business website if you have one and also you can put in your EIN, your tax ID if you have one that's also optional and hit save. Now after you follow that process, you're going to get redirected to something that looks like this. This is going to be your meta-business suite. Now meta-business suite is different than meta-business manager and it's confusing because they are closely related but you're going to see something that looks like this. Now what I want you to do is actually not access everything here in meta-business suite. It's a little bit more clunky and they're still trying to kind of I think perfect this process and system.

## [06:00 - 09:01]

because if not they're going to lock you out of it. Another way that you could add your payment and billing and retroactively go in here and update this if you want to is you go into billing and payments and if you click on this right it's going to give you specific accounts ad accounts as well as the balance you have and the payment methods that you have to pay. So you can see that I've already gone in here and added this amix and I've associated it to this ad account so when I actually go to launch my campaigns and run my ads it's going to default to that primary payment method and if we have a secondary or tertiary payment method we want to add even better we could go in here and hit add payment method you'd select your country your currency your time zone you'd hit next you'd add your payment method if it's a debit card credit card they accept paypal you also accept ad credit if you have one like a voucher and then you go here and add in your card details hit name on the card your card number your month year your CVV and hit save and if it's all correct boom you'd see that payment method show up right here inside of your payment methods in your settings. Now at any given point in time if we want to get back to the main settings dashboard we were currently at we just go to the very bottom here and we can hit business settings and it's a gear icon and we're right back to where we started. So we've done set up our page we make sure that we've added our self as the person of course it's our account that's associated with our email address we've gone in here and actually set up our ad account we've associated the payment method the billing for that ad account and if we want to now we can go and actually add an Instagram account now the way we're going to add an Instagram account is we're going to click on Instagram account and we're going to hit add and what we're going to do now is we're going to hit connect your Instagram account and it's going to open up an Instagram pop up where you would log in to your Instagram you're going to go here you're going to add your phone number username or your email address associated with your Instagram account you're going to select password and then you're going to hit log in and when you do that if you have your account set up correctly you're automatically going to see this Instagram account show up here. Now the reason why that's important is because if we want to run ads on Instagram Instagram as a placement we can still run ads on Instagram if we don't have an Instagram account added but it's going to show up as your business page on Facebook and what we have found and what we have seen is that Instagram ads that show an actual Instagram account as the sponsored person that's running the ad has a much better click through rate and oftentimes a much better performance and it can also help grow your Instagram page as well because obviously the more money you're spending on your ads showing up as your Instagram profile as the sponsor of that ad the chance of them going through clicking in and seeing your Instagram profile is a lot higher which means in turn you're getting a lot more eyeballs to your page and obviously a lot more followers so I would recommend highly that you add an Instagram account you don't need to but it is a bonus. Now also if you want to run ads to WhatsApp same thing here if you have a WhatsApp account you would add this here you'd enter in your phone number you'd hit continue and you would just basically grant your metabusiness manager access to WhatsApp once again you don't have to but it is a bonus especially if you're running WhatsApp ads. Now the next most important thing that we have to do here is we've got to go to data sources

## [18:08 - 19:37]

manager account and think of the consequences if somebody hacks into this account and they access everything that there is to know and access about your business they don't have your credit card your metapixel and they also can go and create ads and what they're going to do is they're going to rack up ad spend sending traffic on your dime to their pages and they can get ugly in a hurry I've seen horror stories of past clients who have had tens of thousands of dollars spent in days and they have no way of going back and accessing that information getting back that spend once the damage is done the damage is done so the last thing that we have to do here to set up your meta business manager account is to go in and set up your two factor authentication I recommend doing a cell phone number um and just having meta send you texts every time you get in it's a little bit annoying but I promise it's worth it for the extra layer of security so there it is you're ready to rock and roll you've got your facebook business page created you've got your meta business manager account set up you set up your ad account you've added a payment method to that ad account you've added a facebook business page you've maybe added an instagram page in a whatsapp page or profile if you have one you've also created a data set added yourself as the owner and admin of that data set gone in and managed and optimized your pixel you've created the pixel code you've installed that pixel code hopefully on your website or your landing page you've tested for the events you've gone into the event setup tool added and set up any other conversion events that you want to optimize and track for on your landing page and now we're ready to actually set up and create our campaign now this is where it starts to get fun
